snippets.title: Top-down DP — Fibonacci with lru_cache
snippets.explanation: The natural recursion + a memo decorator turns O(2ⁿ) into O(n).

snippets.title: Bottom-up DP — Fibonacci in O(1) space
snippets.explanation: We never need more than the last two values — O(n) time, O(1) space.

snippets.title: Edit distance — bottom-up O(m·n)
snippets.explanation: Each cell uses three previously computed neighbours. Walk the table to extract the actual edit script.

snippets.title: Edit distance — rolling rows, O(n) space
snippets.explanation: Because each row depends only on the previous one, keeping two rows is enough — drops space from O(m·n) to O(n).

snippets.title: 0/1 knapsack — bottom-up O(n·W)
snippets.explanation: The 0/1 variant has no greedy solution; DP is required for correctness.

snippets.title: Coin change — minimum coins
snippets.code:
def min_coins(coins, target):
    INF = float('inf')
    dp = [0] + [INF] * target
    for t in range(1, target + 1):
        dp[t] = min((dp[t - c] + 1 for c in coins if c <= t), default=INF)
    return dp[target] if dp[target] < INF else None

print(min_coins([1, 3, 4], 6))   # 2  (3 + 3)  — beats the greedy answer of 3
snippets.explanation: DP correctly solves coin change for any denomination set, including the cases where greedy fails.
